data.title: CS Disco Appoints Cybersecurity Veteran Andre Mintz to Board
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

CS Disco ( (LAW) ) has issued an update.

On July 22, 2026, CS Disco, Inc. expanded its board of directors from eight to nine members and appointed veteran cybersecurity and privacy executive Andre Mintz as a Class II director, with a term running through the company’s 2029 annual meeting of stockholders. The board determined Mintz is independent under New York Stock Exchange rules, and his extensive experience at Meta Platforms, Newport Group, Microsoft, Reuters and other organizations further strengthens CS Disco’s governance depth in data security and privacy.

Mintz’s appointment follows a career that includes senior information security and privacy roles across technology and financial services, as well as board positions at Q2 Holdings, the Cloud Security Alliance and Absolute Software Corporation. As a non-employee director, he will receive a combination of restricted stock units and cash retainers under CS Disco’s standard compensation policy and has entered into the company’s customary indemnification agreement, underscoring the firm’s adherence to established corporate governance and director protection practices.

The most recent analyst rating on (LAW) stock is a Buy
with a $6.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CS Disco stock,
see the LAW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LAW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LAW is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by persistent unprofitability and ongoing cash burn despite strong gross margins and revenue growth, resulting in a below-average financial performance profile. The latest earnings call adds support via a Q1 beat and raised FY guidance with improving adjusted EBITDA, but technicals remain weak with the stock below key moving averages and negative MACD, and valuation is difficult to assess due to a negative P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on LAW stock,
click here.

More about CS Disco

CS Disco, Inc. operates in the legal technology industry, providing cloud-based software that helps law firms, corporate legal departments and other legal professionals manage electronic discovery, document review and related litigation workflows. The company focuses on using artificial intelligence and data analytics to streamline legal processes and improve efficiency for its customers.

data.title: Greenland Mines Adopts Short-Term Stockholder Rights Plan
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Greenland Mines ( (GRML) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, Greenland Mines Ltd’s board approved a limited-duration stockholder rights plan, effective immediately and structured as a dividend of one right for each common share to holders of record on August 7, 2026. The plan will generally make the rights exercisable only if an investor or group acquires 15% or more of the company’s common stock, with provisions that allow existing large holders to be grandfathered subject to strict limits on further share accumulation.

Once triggered, each right permits holders other than the acquiring person to buy Greenland Mines shares at a 50% discount to market value, or to purchase shares of an acquirer on similar terms if the company is sold or its assets are substantially transferred. The rights, which expire on July 22, 2027 unless earlier redeemed, exchanged or terminated following a failed shareholder approval, are intended to deter coercive takeover tactics, preserve the board’s negotiating flexibility and protect shareholder value without blocking fair acquisition proposals.

More about Greenland Mines

Greenland Mines Ltd is a Nasdaq-listed company operating in both mining and biotech. Its mining division focuses on the Skaergaard precious metals project in southeast Greenland and, pending completion of a transaction, the Sarfartoq Nd-Pr rare earths project in southwest Greenland. The biotech division is advancing Klotho’s KLTO-202 candidate for ALS, as part of a broader strategy to build a multi-asset platform across rare earth magnet materials, precious metals and midstream processing linked to a North Atlantic Critical Metals Corridor.

This dual-focus model positions Greenland Mines to connect Greenland-based critical mineral resources with allied downstream jurisdictions and industrial infrastructure. By combining resource development with biotech innovation, the company aims to diversify revenue streams and increase strategic relevance in both the global energy transition supply chain and emerging therapeutic markets.

Average Trading Volume: 5,042,156

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $37.52M

The latest update is out from Franklin Resources ( (BEN) ).

Effective July 21, 2026, Franklin Resources’ board approved one-time special equity retention awards for CEO Jennifer M. Johnson and three Co-Presidents, alongside carry interest allocations for the CEO and Executive Chairman Gregory E. Johnson. The move aims to secure the firm’s core leadership team amid competitive demand for senior talent in asset management and to align their pay more closely with the firm’s evolving private markets and alternative investment platforms.

Each executive equity award, valued at about $15 million, is split evenly between performance stock units with a three-year performance period and restricted stock units with five-year cliff vesting, with strict forfeiture and clawback provisions to ensure long-term accountability. The carry incentives for the CEO and Executive Chairman are fully at-risk, vest over five years and pay out only if flagship private markets and alternative funds exceed hurdle rates, reinforcing a stronger link between leadership rewards, fund performance and long-term shareholder returns.

ClearSign Combustion ( (CLIR) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 21, 2026, ClearSign Technologies secured a waiver from its underwriter, Newbridge Securities, temporarily lifting certain restrictions on issuing and selling capital stock so it could complete a private stock sale to Otter Capital, an existing shareholder holding more than 5% of the company. The waiver, which leaves the rest of the underwriting agreement intact, runs through the earlier of the private sale’s completion or July 31, 2026, underscoring the underwriter’s consent to this targeted fundraising.

On July 21, 2026, the company entered into a stock purchase agreement with Otter Capital for a private placement of 500,000 restricted common shares at $3.54 per share, raising gross proceeds of $1.77 million, with the deal closing on July 22, 2026. ClearSign plans to use the funds for working capital, research and development, marketing, sales and general corporate purposes, signaling a modest but supportive capital infusion from a long-time investor that strengthens its balance sheet without a public offering.

Empery Digital ( (EMPD) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 20, 2026, Empery Digital closed a $20 million preferred equity investment in Cardinal Data Power, securing an 8% stake in the Hunt Properties-affiliated developer of behind-the-meter powered data center campuses. The financing forms part of CDP’s $70 million Series A round, led by Hood River Capital Management, and is intended to support CDP’s inaugural hyperscaler-targeted campus in West Texas, alongside a broader development pipeline across West Texas and West Virginia.

The West Texas site spans more than 3,500 acres, with Phase I designed for a 750 MW data center campus that aims to deliver first power in 2027, ramp to roughly 1 GW of gross capacity by 2029, and allow expansion beyond 5 GW over time. By deepening its strategic partnership with Hunt Properties and gaining exposure to large-scale AI and high-performance computing infrastructure, Empery Digital is reinforcing its shift from a pure bitcoin treasury focus toward diversified digital infrastructure, potentially enhancing its positioning in the power-intensive AI data center market.

Esquire Financial Holdings reported that for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, net income rose 9.2% year over year to $13.0 million, or $1.49 per diluted share, with adjusted earnings of $14.0 million, or $1.60 per share, despite merger-related expenses and an elevated credit loss provision. The bank delivered a 5.96% net interest margin, industry-leading returns on assets and equity, 27.3% loan growth driven largely by litigation-related lending, 22.3% deposit growth, solid asset quality, strong capital ratios, and stable noninterest income, while confirming it has received all regulatory approvals to close its acquisition of Signature Bancorporation on August 1, 2026, expanding its presence in Chicago and the broader Midwest.

Management highlighted continued efficiency with a 50.1% efficiency ratio, robust on- and off-balance-sheet liquidity, and growing sweep-related fee income, positioning Esquire for sustained growth. The company also notched multiple industry recognitions, including top rankings in law firm funding and community banking performance, reinforcing its niche positioning in litigation finance and merchant acquiring and signaling potential benefits for shareholders and clients as the Signature merger integrates a Chicago-based platform into Esquire’s national franchise.

data.title: HF Foods Expands Internationally with Searay Foods Acquisition
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from HF Foods Group ( (HFFG) ).

HF Foods Group Inc., a leading U.S. distributor of international foodservice products to Asian restaurants and related customers, is expanding its footprint beyond its domestic base by acquiring Canadian seafood specialist Searay Foods and related entities. Searay, a major importer and distributor of branded ethnic frozen seafood across North America, will continue to operate under its existing management as a subsidiary, giving HF Foods a stronger presence in the seafood category and access to the Vancouver metro market.

On July 17, 2026, HF Foods agreed to acquire 100% of Searay Foods Inc., Morgan Foods Inc., and Searay Foods USA Inc. for approximately CAD$47.9 million, or about five times Searay’s projected 2025 adjusted EBITDA, funded with a mix of cash and stock and including potential earnout payments. The deal, announced via press release on July 23, 2026 and expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 subject to regulatory approvals, marks HF Foods’ first international expansion and is projected to be immediately accretive to margins and earnings, reinforcing its acquisition-led growth strategy and targeted improvement in consolidated EBITDA margins.

The most recent analyst rating on (HFFG) stock is a Buy
with a $6.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on HF Foods Group stock,
see the HFFG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HFFG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HFFG is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (thin margins, recent losses, and deteriorating free cash flow) despite improved leverage and positive operating cash flow. Earnings-call commentary provides a moderate offset with signs of operational progress and a near-term return to GAAP profitability, but near-term margin and cost headwinds remain. Limited technical data and a negative P/E constrain confidence in trend and valuation support, while corporate events are mixed with liquidity positives but governance pressure.

To see Spark’s full report on HFFG stock,
click here.

More about HF Foods Group

HF Foods Group Inc. is a Nasdaq-listed marketer and distributor of fresh produce, frozen and dry foods, and non-food products, serving primarily Asian restaurants and other foodservice customers across the United States. Headquartered in Las Vegas, the company targets growing demand for Asian cuisine, leveraging a nationwide distribution network and sourcing relationships in the U.S. and Asia.

Searay Foods Inc., founded in 2000 and based in Richmond, British Columbia, is a Canadian importer and distributor of branded ethnic and specialty frozen seafood serving retail, wholesale, and restaurant customers across North America. It sources from more than 80 global suppliers and sells through six proprietary brands focused on specialty frozen seafood offerings.

data.title: Norfolk Southern Posts Record Revenue but Lower Profitability
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Norfolk Southern ( (NSC) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Norfolk Southern reported second-quarter 2026 results that showed record railway operating revenues of $3.5 billion, up 11% year over year on 4% higher volumes and stronger fuel surcharge revenues. Despite the top-line strength, income from railway operations fell 4% to $1.1 billion and diluted EPS declined 4% to $3.26, while the operating ratio deteriorated to 67.6% from 62.2% a year earlier.

Excluding merger-related expenses, restructuring and other charges, and impacts from the Eastern Ohio incident, adjusted income from railway operations rose 5% to $1.2 billion and adjusted EPS increased 7% to $3.52, though the adjusted operating ratio of 65.5% was still 210 basis points worse than last year. Management highlighted improving demand across key markets and emphasized safety and disciplined execution, but acknowledged higher fuel expense, which alongside fuel surcharge growth contributed a notable headwind to efficiency metrics and led the company to raise its 2026 adjusted operating expense guidance to $8.8–$8.9 billion.

The most recent analyst rating on (NSC) stock is a Buy
with a $365.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Norfolk Southern stock,
see the NSC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NSC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NSC is a Neutral.

NSC scores highest on financial quality (strong margins and cash conversion) but is held back by leverage and a mature/slow-growth revenue profile. The latest earnings call was constructive on cost discipline and efficiency, yet near-term fuel and demand/mix pressures remain. Technicals are mixed with softer short-term momentum, and valuation looks somewhat expensive for the current growth backdrop.

To see Spark’s full report on NSC stock,
click here.

More about Norfolk Southern

Norfolk Southern Corporation is a major U.S. freight railroad operating a 22-state transportation network with the most extensive intermodal network in the eastern United States. The company hauls roughly 7 million carloads annually, spanning commodities from agriculture to consumer goods, and connects to every major container port on the Atlantic coast as well as key ports on the Gulf Coast and Great Lakes, helping customers avoid significant carbon emissions by shipping via rail.

Founded in 1827 through predecessor lines, Norfolk Southern serves a majority of the country’s population and manufacturing base and positions itself as a critical backbone of the U.S. economy. Its franchise emphasizes reliable, safe rail service and sustainability, underpinning its appeal to shippers and investors across diversified industrial and consumer markets.

Average Trading Volume: 1,112,043

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $74.41B

The latest announcement is out from Liveperson ( (LPSN) ).

On July 23, 2026, LivePerson issued a shareholder letter and launched a dedicated microsite and FAQ outlining the benefits of its previously announced acquisition by SoundHound AI. The company is urging investors to vote in favor of the deal at a special meeting scheduled for August 20, 2026, emphasizing that uncast votes will effectively count against the transaction and that approval requires a majority of all outstanding shares.

The board said it had evaluated 66 potential counterparties and concluded the SoundHound tie-up was the best available option versus remaining independent, given LivePerson’s business and financial risks. The transaction offers stock-based consideration that implied a roughly 22% premium at announcement, exposure to a larger combined conversational AI platform with greater scale and a stronger balance sheet, and a negotiated restructuring of LivePerson’s secured debt, whose face value currently exceeds the deal’s total consideration, making noteholder concessions pivotal to preserving equity value for shareholders.

data.description:

Popular ( (BPOP) ) has provided an update.

Popular, Inc. reported unaudited second-quarter 2026 net income of $278 million, up 13% from the prior quarter and 32% year over year, with basic and diluted EPS of $4.35 and return on tangible common equity rising to 17%. Results were driven by higher net interest income, solid fee revenue, deposit and loan growth, and strong capital generation, while maintaining net interest margin of 3.66%, stable funding costs and improved capital ratios, as the bank returned $174 million to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.

Credit quality remained generally sound, with non-performing loans decreasing quarter over quarter and an allowance for credit losses equal to 1.97% of total loans, even as net charge-offs increased from a year earlier. The company announced a July 23, 2026 capital plan including a higher common dividend beginning in the fourth quarter of 2026 and a new share repurchase authorization, alongside a leadership transition in which CEO Javier D. Ferrer will retire on August 31, 2026 and CFO Jorge J. García will assume the CEO role on September 1, 2026, prompting a reshuffle of key finance and risk positions.

The most recent analyst rating on (BPOP) stock is a Buy
with a $201.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Popular stock,

data.title: Popular Announces CEO Retirement and Leadership Transition
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Popular ( (BPOP) ).

Popular announced on July 23, 2026 that President and CEO Javier D. Ferrer will retire effective August 31, 2026, ending a tenure that began in 2014 in various leadership roles and as CEO in July 2025. In recognition of his 2026 service, the board’s Talent and Compensation Committee approved a prorated $1.62 million short-term cash incentive and a $2.6 million restricted-stock award with a one-year vesting period, alongside up to three years of continued medical coverage and a one-year post-retirement non-compete and non-solicitation.

Ferrer will also leave the board on his retirement date but will remain engaged through a one-year consulting agreement, earning a $100,000 monthly fee to support the CEO transition and broader business initiatives. The leadership change is framed as orderly and non-contentious, aimed at preserving continuity while leveraging Ferrer’s experience during the handover.

Effective September 1, 2026, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Jorge J. García will become President, CEO and a board member, extending his long career at Popular spanning senior finance, accounting and strategic roles across Puerto Rico and the mainland U.S. García’s promotion underscores the board’s reliance on internal talent with deep institutional knowledge, while confirming there were no special arrangements, family ties or related-party transactions influencing his selection.

In a coordinated reshuffle, Chief Risk Officer Lidio V. Soriano will assume the role of Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, and Luis F. Sousa, currently head of the Credit Risk Management Division, will become Executive Vice President and Chief Risk Officer, both effective September 1, 2026. The appointments highlight a long-planned succession strategy that elevates seasoned risk and finance leaders, reinforcing Popular’s oversight capabilities at a time of heightened regulatory and market scrutiny.

Chairman Richard L. Carrión emphasized Ferrer’s role in shaping Popular’s strategic direction and its Transformation program, which focused on more personalized services, higher employee performance and sustainable profitable growth. He and other executives portrayed García, Soriano and Sousa as experienced leaders whose promotions reflect the depth of Popular’s management bench, suggesting minimal disruption and continued execution of the company’s strategic plan.

The company also noted separately that it released second quarter 2026 financial results on the same day and scheduled a conference call to discuss performance with investors. By pairing earnings disclosures with the leadership transition announcement, Popular signaled an intent to reassure markets about management continuity, capital and risk discipline, and the stability of its long-term strategic trajectory.

The most recent analyst rating on (BPOP) stock is a Buy
with a $201.00 price target.

More about Popular

Popular, Inc., traded on NASDAQ as BPOP, is the leading financial institution in Puerto Rico by assets and deposits and ranks among the top 50 U.S. bank holding companies by assets. Through its main subsidiary Banco Popular de Puerto Rico and mainland unit Popular Bank, it provides retail, mortgage and commercial banking, auto and equipment financing, and broker-dealer and insurance services across Puerto Rico, the U.S. and British Virgin Islands, and select U.S. states.

Founded in 1893, the bank operates a diversified franchise spanning consumer, commercial and leasing products, with branches in New York, New Jersey and Florida supporting its mainland strategy. Its structure combines traditional banking with specialized financial services, positioning Popular as a key player in the Caribbean and an established mid-sized banking group in the broader U.S. market.

data.title: Shinhan Financial Group to Issue KRW Write-Down Contingent Capital Securities for Basel III Capital
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Shinhan Financial Group Co ( (SHG) ).

On July 23, 2026, Shinhan Financial Group’s board approved the issuance of KRW-denominated write-down contingent capital securities to bolster its Basel III regulatory capital. The planned Additional Tier 1 instruments, structured as perpetual securities with call options after five years, initially total KRW 270 billion, with flexibility to increase up to KRW 400 billion depending on demand.

The securities include a non-viability trigger under Korea’s Act on the Structural Improvement of the Financial Industry, allowing full write-off of principal and accrued returns if the group is designated an insolvent financial institution. This move strengthens Shinhan’s loss-absorbing capital base, supports ongoing compliance with capital requirements, and may reassure regulators and investors about its capacity to absorb stress without immediate recourse to public funds or common equity issuance.

data.title: Shinhan Financial Group to Cancel Up to KRW 700 Billion in Treasury Shares After Buyback
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Shinhan Financial Group Co ( (SHG) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 23, 2026, Shinhan Financial Group’s board approved the cancellation of 6,698,565 treasury common shares, representing stock with a face value of KRW 5,000 per share and an estimated total cancellation amount of KRW 700 billion. The move covers one treasury share acquired via a past comprehensive share exchange and the bulk of shares to be repurchased on the Korea Exchange between July 31 and October 22, 2026, under rules requiring cancellation within one year of acquisition.

The company stressed that while the number of issued shares will fall from its current 469,450,239 common shares, the total capital will remain unchanged because the cancellation is executed within profits available for dividends under Article 343 of the Korean Commercial Code. This large-scale share reduction is poised to enhance per-share metrics and signals a shareholder-friendly capital return strategy, though the final number of shares and cancellation amount may vary with market prices and will be confirmed in a later filing.
